name: INS Khukri Memorial
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
description: A solemn and beautifully situated memorial paying tribute to the officers and sailors of the Indian Naval Ship Khukri, which sank during the 1971 Indo-Pak war.
history: The INS Khukri was torpedoed by a Pakistani submarine on December 9, 1971, off the coast of Diu. The memorial features a scaled model of the frigate encased in glass facing the sea.
famousFor: Naval war memorial, glass-encased ship model, and sunset views.
